eras.name: Indus Valley Civilization
eras.description: Earliest evidence of cotton cultivation and spinning. Clothing was largely unstitched, consisting of wrap-around garments and elaborate jewelry made of terracotta, beads, and gold.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Ancient Indus Valley fashion, a woman wearing a fine cotton wrap-around lower garment and a draped shoulder cloth, adorned with multi-strand carnelian bead necklaces and terracotta bangles, realistic historical reconstruction.

eras.name: Mauryan and Shunga Period
eras.description: Refinement of the draped silhouette with a focus on fine muslins. This era saw the evolution of the three-piece attire: the lower garment, the veil, and the chest band.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Mauryan era Indian attire, high-ranking official wearing a finely pleated translucent cotton dhoti and an embroidered sash, intricate headgear, stone sculpture aesthetic, golden hour lighting.

eras.name: Mughal Imperial Era
eras.description: A fusion of Persian aesthetics with Indian craftsmanship. Introduction of tailored garments, heavy brocades, and floral motifs like the 'Buta' (paisley).
eras.aiImagePrompt: Mughal royalty fashion, a nobleman wearing a silk Jama with gold Zardozi embroidery, a jeweled turban with a kalgi feather, and a cashmere pashmina shawl, opulent palace background.

eras.name: British Raj and Swadeshi Movement
eras.description: The rise of Victorian influence on Indian silhouettes, followed by the Swadeshi movement which promoted hand-spun Khadi as a symbol of political resistance.
eras.aiImagePrompt: 1920s India, a woman in a hand-spun Khadi saree representing the Swadeshi movement, simple and dignified, sepia-toned vintage photography style.

eras.name: Post-Independence and Bollywood Rise
eras.description: Cinema became the primary fashion driver. The era saw the experimentation with synthetic fabrics like polyester and the iconic 'Mumtaz' style draped saree.
eras.aiImagePrompt: 1970s Bollywood fashion, a woman in a vibrant orange chiffon saree with a sleeveless blouse and oversized sunglasses, retro cinematic aesthetic.

eras.name: Contemporary Global Fusion
eras.description: Economic liberalization led to the entry of global brands and the rise of the 'Modern Indian' aesthetic—blending traditional drapes with Western tailoring.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Modern Indian high-fashion, a model wearing a structured metallic concept saree with a leather belt and contemporary jewelry, architectural background, high-fashion editorial style.

textiles.name: Banarasi Silk
textiles.description: Fine silk from Varanasi known for gold and silver brocade or 'Zari'.

textiles.name: Khadi
textiles.description: Hand-spun and hand-woven natural fiber cloth, central to India's identity.

textiles.name: Ajrakh
textiles.description: A unique form of block printing using natural dyes, featuring complex geometric patterns.

textiles.name: Chikan
textiles.description: Delicate and artful hand embroidery traditionally done with white thread on cool fabrics.
textiles.technique: Shadow work and various needlework stitches

climate.fashionImplication: High humidity necessitates breathable natural fibers like cotton and linen. The intense heat drives the preference for loose, draped silhouettes over tight-fitting garments.

emergingTrends: Sustainable Luxury and Upcycled Heirloom Textiles
Digital Prints inspired by Indian Folklore
Modest Fashion within the Luxury Segment
notableEvents: The Big Fat Indian Wedding Season
